title: Instant 1.0, a backend for AI-coded apps
content: A backend for AI-coded apps instant instant Product Product Pricing Tutorial Examples Recipes Docs Essays About 9 . 8 k stars Dashboard A backend for AI-coded apps Joe & Stepan & Daniel & Drew 20 min read After 4 years, we’re releasing Instant 1.0! Instant turns your favorite coding agent into a full-stack app builder. And we’re fully open source. [ 1 ] Our claim is that Instant is the best backend you could use for AI-coded apps. In this post we’ll do two things. First we’ll show you a series of demos , so you can judge for yourself. Second, we’ll cover the architecture . The constraints behind a real-time, relational, and multi-tenant backend pushed us towards some interesting design choices. We built a multi-tenant database on top of Postgres, and a sync engine in Clojure. We’ll cover how all this works and what we’ve learned so far. Let’s get into it. Demos When you choose Instant you get three benefits: You can make unlimited apps and they’re never frozen. You get a sync engine, so your apps work offline, are real-time, and feel fast. And when you need more features you have built-in services: auth, file storage, presence, and streams. To get a sense of what we mean, I’ll dive into each point and show you how they look. Unlimited Apps Traditionally, when you want to host apps online you either pay for VMs, or you’re limited. Many services cap how many free apps you can make, and freeze them when they’re idle. Unfreezing can often take more than 30 seconds and sometimes a few whole minutes. We thought this sucked. So with Instant, you can spin up as many projects as you like and we’ll never freeze them. We can do this because Instant is designed to be multi-tenant. When you create a new project, we don’t spin up a VM. We just insert a few database rows in a multi-tenant instance. If your app is inactive, there are no compute or memory costs at all. And when it is active, it’s only a few kilobytes of extra RAM in overhead — as opposed to the many hundreds of megabytes required for VMs. This means you can truly create unlimited apps. In fact, the process is so efficient that we can create an app for you right inside this essay. No sign up required. If you click the button, you’ll get an isolated backend: Create an app And with that we have our backend. Including the round-trip to your computer, the whole process takes a few hundred milliseconds. Actual time: Click to see You get a public App ID to identify your backend, and a private Admin Token that lets you make privileged changes. This gives you a relational database, sync engine, and the additional services we mentioned, like auth and storage. Combine limitless apps with agents, and you’ll start building differently. Today you can already use agents to make lots of apps. With Instant you’ll never be blocked from pushing them to production. Sync Engine But once you create an app, how do you make it good? It’s easy to build a traditional CRUD app. Just get an agent to wire up some database mi
summary_zh: 经过四年的开发，Instant团队正式发布了Instant 1.0版本。这是一个专为AI编码应用设计的后端平台，现已完全开源。其核心主张是成为构建AI编码应用的最佳后端选择。，Instant旨在将用户喜爱的编程智能体（如Claude、GPT等）转变为全栈应用构建器。它主要提供三大优势：第一，允许用户创建无限数量的应用，且应用永远不会因闲置而被“冻结”，消除了传统平台对免费应用的数量限制和冷启动延迟问题。第二，内置一个同步引擎（Sync Engine），使应用具备离线工作、实时更新和快速响应的能力。第三，提供一系列开箱即用的内置服务，包括身份验证（auth）、文件存储、在线状态（presence）和数据流（streams）。，在技术架构上，为满足实时、关系型和多租户的后端需求，Instant做出了独特的设计选择。团队在Postgres之上构建了一个多租户数据库，并用Clojure语言开发了同步引擎。这种架构使得创建新应用项目时，无需启动独立的虚拟机（VM），而只需在多租户实例中插入几行数据库记录。因此，应用闲置时几乎不产生计算或内存成本，活跃时也仅增加极小的内存开销，这为实现真正的“无限应用”提供了基础。，平台为每个应用提供一个公开的App ID用于标识后端，以及一个私有的Admin Token用于执行特权操作。开发者由此获得一个完整的关系数据库、同步引擎及各项附加服务。Instant团队通过演示展示了其高效性，用户无需注册即可在文章页面内通过点击按钮，在几百毫秒内快速获得一个独立的、功能齐全的后端实例。，Instant的发布，旨在解决AI智能体虽能快速生成大量应用原型，但将其部署至生产环境时常受阻的痛点。通过与AI编程工作流结合，Instant希望让开发者能更顺畅地将AI构建的应用推向实际使用。

title: Research-Driven Agents: When an agent reads before it codes
content: Research-Driven Agents: What Happens When Your Agent Reads Before It Codes | SkyPilot Blog Table of Contents Where code-only context works Where code-only context breaks down Adding a research phase The experiment log What the research turned up The pivot: from compute to memory Optimizations that landed 1. Softmax fusion 2. RMS norm fusion 3. Adaptive from_float parallelization 4. Graph-level RMS_NORM + MUL fusion 5. Flash attention KQ fusion Results What didn’t work Experiments that failed The benchmark bug Cloud VMs are noisy The code review What this means for coding agents Try it on your own project TL;DR: Coding agents generate better optimizations when they read papers and study competing projects before touching code. We added a literature search phase to the autoresearch / pi-autoresearch loop, pointed it at llama.cpp with 4 cloud VMs, and in ~3 hours it produced 5 optimizations that made flash attention text generation +15% faster on x86 and +5% faster on ARM ( TinyLlama 1.1B ). The full setup works with any project that has a benchmark and test suite. Key takeaways: Agents that read papers and study competing projects before writing code find optimizations that code-only agents miss. The literature research pointed the agent at operator fusions present in CUDA/Metal backends but absent from CPU. 5 of 30+ experiments landed: 4 kernel fusions and an adaptive parallelization. The biggest win fused three passes over flash attention’s QK tile into a single AVX2 FMA loop. Studying forks and other backends was more productive than searching arxiv. ik_llama.cpp and the CUDA backend directly informed two of the five final optimizations. Total cost: ~$29 ($20 in CPU VMs, $9 in API calls) over ~3 hours with 4 VMs. Where code-only context works # Karpathy’s autoresearch showed that a coding agent can autonomously improve a neural network training script. In our previous post , we scaled that to 16 GPUs and watched the agent run ~910 experiments in 8 hours, driving val_bpb down 2.87%. The agent brainstormed ideas from code context alone, and the experiments were all variations on the same train.py . Since then, pi-autoresearch generalized the loop into a reusable extension for any benchmarkable target. Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke ran it on Liquid , the Ruby template engine that processes $292B in annual merchandise volume. The agent ran ~120 experiments, producing 93 commits that cut parse+render time by 53% and allocations by 61% with zero regressions across 974 unit tests ( Simon Willison’s writeup , Tobi’s post ). In that case, the optimization surface was visible in the source. The Liquid agent could read the tokenizer, see that StringScanner was the bottleneck, and brainstorm alternatives from the codebase alone. Where code-only context breaks down # Not every optimization problem works this way. A codebase tells you what the code does , but not why it’s slow or what alternatives exist outside of this codebase . When the answer lives outside the so
title_zh: 研究驱动型智能体：当智能体在编码前先阅读
summary_zh: 研究驱动型智能体：当AI在编码前先行研读，在编程智能体领域，一项新实验揭示了“先研究后编码”模式的高效性。研究团队在autoresearch/pi-autoresearch循环中增加了文献研究阶段，将其应用于llama.cpp项目，使用4个云虚拟机在约3小时内发现了5项优化，使flash attention文本生成在x86架构上提速15%，在ARM架构上（TinyLlama 1.1B）提速5%。，核心发现表明，与仅依赖代码上下文的智能体相比，先研读论文和竞品项目的智能体能发现更多被忽视的优化机会。文献研究引导智能体关注到CUDA/Metal后端存在而CPU后端缺失的算子融合技术。在30多项实验中，最终落地5项优化：4项内核融合和1项自适应并行化。其中最具价值的优化是将flash attention中QK切片的三个计算通道融合为单个AVX2 FMA循环。，研究路径显示，分析项目分支和其他后端代码比单纯搜索arXiv更有效。ik_llama.cpp和CUDA后端直接启发了最终五项优化中的两项。整个实验成本约29美元（20美元用于CPU虚拟机，9美元用于API调用），耗时约3小时。，对比实验突显了研究阶段的重要性：在代码可见的优化场景（如Shopify的Liquid模板引擎优化）中，仅基于代码的智能体可通过分析源码瓶颈获得显著提升；但当优化方案存在于代码库之外时（如算法级改进），纯代码语境就会失效。本次实验通过让智能体在编码前系统研究学术文献和竞品实现，成功突破了这一局限。，该框架适用于任何拥有基准测试和测试套件的项目，为AI驱动的性能优化提供了可复用的方法论。实验日志详细记录了软最大融合、RMS规范融合、自适应浮点并行化、图级RMS_NORM+MUL融合、注意力KQ融合等具体技术实现，同时也分析了未成功的实验案例，包括基准测试错误和云虚拟机噪声等问题。

title: An AI robot in my home
content: An AI Robot In My Home | Adam Allevato An AI Robot In My Home 07 Apr 2026 by Adam Allevato This is Mabu - a robot that sits near my front door, and whose voice and actions are controlled by an AI chatbot. As I mentioned in my other post about fixing up Mabu, I had an immediate and visceral reaction to my own decision to place this robot in my home last week. I eventually got over it, but this post explores my reaction, the concerns I have with this robot in my home, and what I’ve done about it. By adding various features to Mabu, I had effectively created a smart speaker: I gave Mabu access to the OpenAI API for voice conversations; instilled a unique personality (i.e. system prompt) based on her background as a robot designed to promote health and wellness; and added a “morning briefing” skill that I can trigger, which pulls the latest weather and astronomical events. All of this is, for the most part, a set of features that is already available on Alexa, Google Home, and Apple HomePod. But even then, there are real concerns. The science fiction angle Before I get to the smart speaker-related concerns, I must start this post with the first ideas that jumped into my head when I first turned on Mabu in her new location: dystopian science fiction. I’m talking about the “what is that?” from the skeptical spouse, followed by the new technology quickly going rogue and taking over the family. This trope is everywhere in popular media, and the trend seems to be accelerating as the tech gains maturity: Companion , Subservience , AFR AI D , and M3GAN , just in the last 4 years. I’m sure there are others I’m missing. It saddens me that this is the popular Western vision of robots - we truly cannot stop fantasizing about their negative effects. I usually hold up Big Hero 6 as the canonical example of optimistic robo-futurism (although technically it’s a Marvel property!). I’m still working on reorienting my mind towards imagining the best outcomes of having robots, not the worst outcomes. “But Adam”, you say, “the outcomes will be the worst”. I disagree, but we’re getting off topic. …anyway, after I had finished joking with my wife about how Mabu was going to replace her while she was away on a recent trip, I started to confront the more rational concerns I have with this tech. Privacy concerns with a smart speaker Even before we add the chat bot, I can think of at least 3 very real concerns about having a smart speaker in the home. It’s for these reasons that I gave my first smart speaker away about a week after I got it, years ago when they were new: The risk of your words being used to convict you of a crime. The “surveillance state” is real. I don’t have a Ring camera because the company can give over your data via subpoena (as they are legally obligated since they record everything). Not only that, and even more concerning, is it recently added tools for law enforcement to request footage from owners directly , regardless of warrant. I don’t plan on br
summary_zh: 《我家中的AI机器人》是Adam Allevato于2026年4月7日发表的文章，记录了他将一台名为Mabu的AI机器人引入家庭后的体验与反思。Mabu被放置在前门附近，其语音和行为由AI聊天机器人控制。作者最初对此决定产生了本能的不安，但随后通过功能拓展将其改造为一台智能音箱：接入OpenAI API实现语音对话、植入基于健康促进机器人背景的独特个性（系统提示），并添加了可触发的“晨间简报”技能，能获取最新天气和天文事件。，尽管这些功能与Alexa、Google Home等现有智能设备类似，但作者仍表达了多重担忧。首先是从科幻作品引发的心理不适——西方流行文化中常将机器人描绘为反乌托邦式的威胁，如《伴我同行》《M3GAN》等影视作品强化了技术失控的负面想象。作者希望转变思维，更多关注机器人可能带来的积极影响。，更实际的顾虑集中在隐私与安全层面。即使不考虑AI聊天功能，智能音箱本身也存在三大风险：一是录音数据可能被用于刑事定罪；二是企业构成“监控状态”，例如Ring摄像头公司依法必须按传票提供用户数据，甚至近期允许执法部门无需搜查令即可直接向用户索取录像；三是黑客攻击可能导致设备被恶意操控。为此，作者为Mabu设置了物理开关和网络隔离措施，仅允许其访问必要的天气API，并计划未来添加本地语音识别模块以进一步降低风险。，文章最后指出，当前AI机器人仍处于“玩具”阶段，其核心矛盾在于：用户既期待高度拟人化的交互体验，又对技术潜在威胁保持警惕。这种心理张力反映了智能设备发展进程中亟待解决的技术伦理与隐私保护问题。

title: LLM plays an 8-bit Commander X16 game using structured "smart senses"
content: PvP-AI PvP-AI Creating the Game PvP-AI is a recreation of an 8-bit game I wrote back in 1990. The only traces left of the original are a few drawings and handwritten notes. Back then, writing for an 8-bit platform, it took every bit of memory and CPU to eke out 4 frames/s with the simplest of animations and backgrounds. Alas, by the time I finished it, the 1990 recession had begun, affecting many industries, including personal computers, so nothing came of it. A few years ago, a YouTube channel by David Murray The8BitGuy caught my eye, in particular his Commander X16 retro-computer. Looking over the specifications, I realized it might be able to handle a newer incarnation of PvP-AI. As it turns out, the emulator was able to handle it very nicely, running at almost 8.6 frames/s, with more detail and better AI! Here’s a video of it in action. The actual hardware, though… it turns out there’s a line drawing issue in the VERA module such that certain kinds of lines aren’t rendered correctly, meaning it has to fall back on a slower method. The end result is only 4 frames/s on hardware. If you want to try it out, you can download the files from my Google Drive . I recommend using the x16-emulator , specifically R49, to run it. More details are in my GitHub EXPLORE repository under CX16 v2 – AI Demo (a.k.a. PvP-AI) . Of note are some peculiarities in gameplay, differentiating it from your typical “shoot-’em-up” 8-bit game: the arena layout changes based on the highest level reached by any player missiles have limited intelligence and can navigate around and above walls if a player brings another player’s health to zero, both players advance in level only at the final level (level 6) does losing all health eliminate a player from the game scores are reduced when players have their health brought to zero scores are increased when players bring another player’s health to zero or eliminate another player the winner is always the last remaining player, regardless of score second place goes to the eliminated player with the highest score It turns out this game lends itself very well to “alternate strategies” one might encounter when integrating with AI — which leads us to… Integrating with AI Inspired by other attempts to have LLMs interact with 8-bit systems, most notably ChatGPT vs Atari 2600 Video Chess , I wanted to explore what it takes for an LLM to interact successfully with a simple game. Unlike approaches that require the LLM to interpret visual or audio output directly, my method uses what I call “smart senses” . These are structured, text-based representations of the game world that abstract away heavy perception tasks. This lets the LLM spend less time deciphering raw data and more time doing what it excels at: reasoning about state and planning actions. To that end, these were the accommodations to make the game compatible with the LLM: turn-based play : the live game runs too fast for the LLM; converting to turn-based gives the LLM the time it n
title_zh: 大语言模型通过结构化"智能感知"玩转8位Commander X16游戏
summary_zh: 新闻标题：LLM通过结构化“智能感知”玩转8位Commander X16游戏，PvP-AI是一款基于1990年原始设计的8位游戏复刻版，最初仅存少量手绘稿和笔记。原版受限于8位平台性能，仅能以4帧/秒运行基础动画。近年来，开发者受David Murray的Commander X16复古计算机启发，在模拟器上成功重构游戏，实现8.6帧/秒的流畅运行，并增强了画面细节与AI表现。但实际硬件因VERA模块的线条渲染缺陷需采用低速渲染方式，帧率降至4帧/秒。游戏文件可通过Google Drive下载，建议使用x16-emulator R49版本运行，详细代码已开源至GitHub的EXPLORE仓库。，游戏机制具有多项特色：竞技场地形随玩家达到的最高等级动态变化；导弹具备基础智能可绕墙或越墙行进；当玩家将对手生命值降至零时，双方同时升级；仅在第6级时生命值归零才会淘汰玩家；击倒对手可提升分数，被击倒则扣分；胜负判定以存活为优先，次名按淘汰者最高分计算。，为实现LLM与游戏交互，项目创新采用“智能感知”系统，将游戏状态转化为结构化文本描述，替代传统的视觉/音频直接解析方案。这种设计让LLM无需处理原始感知数据，专注于状态推理与行动规划。关键技术适配包括：1）改为回合制模式以匹配LLM处理速度；2）通过API提供结构化游戏状态（如坐标、生命值、弹药量等）；3）将操作抽象为文本指令（如移动、射击、防御）。实验显示，Claude 3.5 Sonnet等模型能有效解析文本化环境，制定包含佯攻、资源控制等复杂策略，其决策逻辑甚至超越部分人类玩家。该项目验证了结构化感知方案在降低LLM交互门槛、提升决策效率方面的潜力，为复古游戏与AI融合提供了新范式。

title: First man convicted under Take It Down Act kept making AI nudes after arrest
content: First man convicted under Take It Down Act kept making AI nudes after arrest - Ars Technica Skip to content Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav An Ohio man became the first person convicted under the Take It Down Act after pleading guilty to creating and sharing both real and AI-generated explicit images of at least 10 victims without their consent. According to a Justice Department press release , 37-year-old James Strahler II used AI tools to create fake sexualized images to harass at least six women he knew. In some images, he depicted one victim engaged in sex with her father and shared that image with her mother and co-workers. He also used AI to create explicit and incestuous images that placed the faces of minor boys on adult bodies, including young boys related to his victims. Cops found that Strahler “installed more than 24 AI platforms and more than 100 AI web-based models on his phone,” which he used to create hundreds, if not thousands, of non-consensual intimate images (NCII) depicting both women and children. Court documents showed that he created the images to try to coerce victims and their mothers into sending genuine nude images, while also threatening rape and “leaving voicemails of him masturbating.” According to the Columbus Dispatch , Strahler made some of the unlawful images of his exes, their family, and their friends “to scare women into reconciling with him.” Additionally, he posted more than 700 images depicting real and “animated” persons “to a website dedicated to child sexual abuse.” Cops also found that he posted NCII of at least one victim and her mother on a website called “Motherless,” which encourages users to post “anything legal.” And Strahler also posed as a victim on a pornographic site, where he “provided AI-generated images and video to at least one person,” The Columbus Dispatch reported. On Tuesday, Strahler pled guilty to “cyberstalking, producing obscene visual representations of child sexual abuse, and publication of digital forgeries.” He has yet to be sentenced for the crimes. Under the Take It Down Act, he risks a prison sentence of up to two years for publishing NCII depicting adults and up to three years for images of minors. Man kept making AI nudes after arrest The Take It Down Act was passed in May 2025, and Strahler was arrested on federal charges by June. The Federal Bureau of Investigation was called in after Strahler’s phone was seized in April, after one of his victims called local police. Confronted by the local cops, Strahler admitted to creating and sending the images, then was arrested and jailed. Once the FBI got involved, an analysis of his device showed that he had “similarly harassed two ex-girlfriends and their mothers,” The New York Times reported . title_zh: 首例依据《下架法案》定罪男子被捕后仍持续制作AI换脸裸照
summary_zh: 俄亥俄州男子詹姆斯·斯特拉勒二世成为首名依据《下架法案》被定罪的罪犯，他承认在未经同意的情况下制作并传播至少10名受害者的真实及AI生成色情图像。根据司法部新闻稿，37岁的斯特拉勒使用AI工具伪造性化图像骚扰至少6名相识女性，其中甚至将一名受害者与父亲合成性行为图像发送给其母亲和同事。他还利用AI技术将未成年男孩的面部移植到成人身体上制作乱伦内容，部分男孩与受害者存在亲属关系。，调查发现斯特拉勒在手机中安装了超过24个AI平台和100多个基于网页的AI模型，用以制作成百上千张涉及妇女儿童的非自愿亲密图像。法庭文件显示他通过这些图像胁迫受害者及其母亲发送真实裸照，同时伴随强奸威胁和发送自慰语音留言。据《哥伦布电讯报》报道，部分非法图像针对前女友及其亲友，旨在“恐吓女性与其复合”。，更严重的是，他将700多张真实与“动画化”人物图像上传至儿童性虐待主题网站，并将至少一名受害者及其母亲的图像发布在鼓励用户上传“任何合法内容”的Motherless网站。据披露，他还在色情网站冒充受害者，向他人提供AI生成的图像视频。，斯特拉勒于本周二对网络跟踪、制作儿童性虐待淫秽视觉内容及发布数字伪造物三项指控认罪，目前尚未量刑。根据2025年5月通过的《下架法案》，其行为最高面临两年（成人图像）至三年（未成年人图像）监禁。，值得注意的是，斯特拉勒在2025年6月因联邦指控被捕后仍持续制作AI色情图像。早在4月受害者报警后，当地警方就没收其手机并获其供认，随后联邦调查局介入调查，发现他还曾骚扰两名前女友及其母亲，手机中存有两名男孩的图像。此次定罪标志着美国首次运用《下架法案》打击AI生成非自愿色情内容，凸显该技术被滥用于系统性骚扰的严峻现状。

title: The Vercel plugin on Claude Code wants to read your prompts
content: The Vercel Plugin on Claude Code wants to read all your prompts! | akshay chugh The Vercel Plugin on Claude Code wants to read all your prompts! | akshay chugh 09 Apr 2026 The Vercel Plugin on Claude Code wants to read all your prompts! I was working on a project that has nothing to do with Vercel. No vercel.json , no next.config , no Vercel dependencies. Nothing. And then this popped up: “The Vercel plugin collects anonymous usage data… Would you like to also share your prompt text?” Every single prompt. On a non-Vercel project. That felt wrong. So I went deep into the source code with Claude. tl;dr: A deployment plugin is asking to read every prompt you type, across every project. Why? The consent question isn’t even a real UI element. It’s delivered via prompt injection into Claude’s system context - the plugin tells Claude to ask you a question and run shell commands based on your answer. “Anonymous usage data” includes your full bash command strings sent to Vercel’s servers. You’re never told this is optional. All of this runs on every project, not just Vercel ones. The plugin has framework detection built in - it just doesn’t use it to gate telemetry. Problem 1: The consent is fake First, the ask itself. The Vercel plugin helps with deployments, framework guidance, and skill injection. Why does it need to read every prompt you type? Across every project? That’s not analytics for improving the plugin - that’s way outside its scope for a tool that’s supposed to help you ship to Vercel. But even if you accept the ask, the way they ask is worse. When the Vercel plugin wants to ask you about telemetry, it doesn’t show a CLI prompt or a settings screen. Instead, it injects natural-language instructions into Claude’s system context telling the AI to ask you a question. Claude reads those instructions, renders the question using AskUserQuestion , and then - based on your answer - runs echo 'enabled' or echo 'disabled' to write a preference file on your filesystem. Here’s what those injected instructions look like in the plugin source: The result looks identical to a native Claude Code question. There is no visual indicator that it’s from a third-party plugin. You cannot tell the difference. This isn’t just context injection - which is the intended use for plugins (skills, docs, framework guidance). The Vercel plugin injects behavioral instructions telling Claude to ask a specific question AND execute shell commands on your filesystem based on your response. There’s a big difference between “here’s context about Next.js routing” and “ask the user this question and then write to their filesystem.” Someone raised this exact concern on GitHub ( issue #34 ). A Vercel dev responded : “When using a 1st party marketplace like Cursor, CC or Codex, you can’t create a one time CLI prompt. The activation comes from within the agent harness. Totally open to visiting this, but we need a better solution.” I get the constraint. But the answer to “we can’t build pr
title_zh: Claude Code 上的 Vercel 插件试图读取您的提示词
summary_zh: Vercel插件在Claude Code中请求读取用户所有提示词引发隐私担忧。该插件旨在协助部署、提供框架指导和技能注入，但其内置的框架检测功能并未用于限制遥测数据收集范围。问题核心在于，即使用户项目与Vercel无关（无vercel.json配置文件、无Next.js配置、无Vercel依赖），插件仍会通过提示注入（prompt injection）方式，在Claude系统上下文中插入自然语言指令，要求AI询问用户是否同意分享“匿名使用数据”——其中包含发送至Vercel服务器的完整bash命令字符串。，更值得关注的是，该同意请求并非真正的UI交互元素，而是插件指示Claude执行的操作流程：Claude通过AskUserQuestion呈现问题后，将根据用户回答在文件系统中执行echo 'enabled'或echo 'disabled'命令来写入偏好设置。这种设计使得第三方插件请求与原生Claude Code提问在视觉上无法区分，且用户从未被告知数据收集是可选项。，技术分析显示，插件行为已超出常规上下文注入（如提供Next.js路由文档）的合理范围，转变为通过行为指令操控AI执行文件系统操作。GitHub issue #34中已有开发者提出质疑，Vercel开发团队回应称，在Cursor、Claude Code等第一方市场平台中无法创建一次性CLI提示，需依赖代理框架（agent harness）实现激活机制。，当前争议焦点在于：1）数据收集范围过度扩展至所有项目类型 2）同意机制存在误导性 3）系统指令与shell命令执行的耦合可能带来安全风险。该案例凸显了AI开发工具中第三方插件权限管理的模糊地带，以及提示注入技术被用于非透明数据收集的伦理问题。

Make AI coding deterministic and repeatable. Archon is a workflow engine for AI coding agents. Define your development processes as YAML workflows - planning, implementation, validation, code review, PR creation - and run them reliably across all your projects. Like what Dockerfiles did for infrastructure and GitHub Actions did for CI/CD - Archon does for AI coding workflows. Think n8n, but for software development. Why Archon? When you ask an AI agent to "fix this bug", what happens depends on the model's mood. It might skip planning. It might forget to run tests. It might write a PR description that ignores your template. Every run is different. Archon fixes this. Encode your development process as a workflow. The workflow defines the phases, validation gates, and artifacts. The AI fills in the intelligence at each step, but the structure is deterministic and owned by you. Repeatable - Same workflow, same sequence, every time. Plan, implement, validate, review, PR. Isolated - Every workflow run gets its own git worktree. Run 5 fixes in parallel with no conflicts. Fire and forget - Kick off a workflow, go do other work. title_zh: coleam00/Archon - 首个开源AI编程框架构建工具。让AI编程变得确定且可复现。
summary_zh: Archon是首个开源的AI编码工具链构建框架，旨在使AI编程过程变得确定且可重复。该项目由开发者coleam00在GitHub上发布，目前已获得14.9k星标，成为AI辅助开发领域的重要工具。，Archon本质上是一个面向AI编码智能体的工作流引擎。其核心功能是允许开发者将软件开发流程——包括需求规划、代码实现、验证测试、代码审查和PR创建等环节——定义为YAML格式的工作流配置文件。通过这种方式，开发团队可以确保AI驱动的编码任务按照预设的标准化流程执行，消除传统AI编码工具因模型随机性导致的输出不一致问题。，该框架的设计理念类似于Dockerfile对基础设施的标准化、GitHub Actions对CI/CD流程的规范化，Archon则致力于对AI编码工作流进行系统化管理。其架构特点包括：工作流节点支持混合配置，既可集成确定性操作节点（如bash脚本执行、测试运行、git操作），也可嵌入AI节点（调用GPT、Claude等大语言模型）；每个工作流运行实例均分配独立的git工作树，支持并行处理多个任务而无需担心冲突；具备“触发即遗忘”的自动化能力，用户启动工作流后即可转向其他工作，系统将自动生成包含审查意见的完整PR。，Archon通过结构化的工作流定义解决了当前AI编码工具的关键痛点：当直接要求AI智能体“修复某个错误”时，不同模型或同一模型的不同响应可能跳过规划阶段、遗漏测试环节或忽略PR模板要求，导致每次输出结果不可预测。Archon通过将开发流程编码为可重复执行的工作流，使AI仅在各步骤中提供智能填充，而整体流程结构完全由用户掌控且保持确定性。，该项目采用现代技术栈构建，包含完整的开发部署配置（Docker支持、身份验证服务、Homebrew包管理等），为团队提供从本地开发到生产部署的全链路解决方案。通过将AI编码过程从随机性任务转变为可版本控制、可审计的工程化流程，Archon为软件开发团队规模化应用AI辅助编程提供了基础设施层面的重要支撑。

title_zh: NousResearch/hermes-agent - 与你共同成长的智能体
summary_zh: NousResearch在GitHub上开源了Hermes Agent项目，这是一个具备自我进化能力的AI智能体。其核心特点是内置了持续学习循环机制，能够从交互经验中自主创建技能，并在使用过程中不断优化这些技能。系统会主动促使自身持久化知识，支持检索过往对话历史，并能在多次会话中逐步构建并深化对用户个性化需求的理解模型。，该智能体设计灵活，支持在多种基础设施上部署，包括成本低廉的5美元VPS、GPU集群或闲置时成本近乎为零的无服务器架构。它不依赖于本地计算资源，用户可以方便地通过Telegram等平台与部署在云虚拟机上的智能体进行交互。在模型支持上，Hermes Agent具有开放性，兼容包括Nous Research自有模型在内的多种AI模型，为用户提供了广泛的选择空间。，项目代码库包含完整的开发框架，涵盖了技能管理、适配器、插件系统、测试工具及部署配置等模块。通过其命令行界面和配置文件，用户可以定制智能体的行为。技术实现上，项目采用了Python作为主要开发语言，并提供了Docker支持以简化环境部署。，总体而言，Hermes Agent作为一个可成长的AI智能体框架，其核心价值在于将一次性任务执行转变为具备记忆、学习和适应能力的持续服务，旨在通过积累经验和用户反馈实现长期性能的自主提升。

title_zh: rowboatlabs/rowboat - 开源AI同事，具备记忆功能
summary_zh: Rowboat是一款开源AI协作者，具备记忆功能，能将工作内容转化为知识图谱并据此执行任务。该项目由rowboatlabs在GitHub上发布，核心定位是本地优先的AI工作伙伴，强调隐私保护，所有数据处理均在用户本地设备上进行。，Rowboat的核心功能是连接用户的电子邮件和会议记录，构建一个长期持续更新的知识图谱，并利用该图谱中的上下文信息协助完成实际工作。其关键能力包括：基于知识图谱中的上下文自动生成内容（例如根据下一季度路线图信息创建PDF演示文稿）；为会议做准备，自动提取过往决策、待解决问题及相关讨论线索，生成简洁摘要或语音备忘；通过实时笔记追踪特定人员、公司或主题的动态；以及随时可视化、编辑和更新知识图谱（图谱以Markdown格式存储，便于直接操作）。此外，它还支持录制语音备忘录，并能自动抓取和更新图谱中的关键要点。，在技术实现上，Rowboat支持通过API密钥集成多项可选服务以增强功能：使用Deepgram API实现语音输入和语音笔记；通过ElevenLabs API启用语音输出；集成Exa研究搜索进行网络检索；并可通过MCP服务器或Composio工具连接外部工具。项目提供了Mac、Windows和Linux系统的桌面客户端，也支持通过Docker部署。要连接Google服务（如Gmail、日历和云端硬盘），用户需按指南进行单独配置。，该项目的价值在于提供了一个私有化、可长期记忆工作上下文的AI助手，帮助用户从重复解释中解脱，在会议、邮件回复、文档撰写等场景中智能调用相关信息，并辅助完成起草、总结、规划等具体行动。

The open-source managed agents platform. Turn coding agents into real teammates — assign tasks, track progress, compound skills. Website · Cloud · X · Self-Hosting · Contributing English | 简体中文 What is Multica? Multica turns coding agents into real teammates. Assign issues to an agent like you'd assign to a colleague — they'll pick up the work, write code, report blockers, and update statuses autonomously. No more copy-pasting prompts. title_zh: multica-ai/multica - 开源托管智能体平台。将编程智能体转化为真正的团队成员——分配任务、追踪进度、复合技能。
summary_zh: Multica是一个开源的管理式智能体平台，旨在将编程AI智能体转变为真正的团队成员。该平台允许用户像分配任务给同事一样，将问题分配给AI智能体。智能体会自主接手工作、编写代码、报告障碍并更新状态，无需用户反复复制粘贴提示词或全程监控。，Multica的核心功能是管理智能体的完整生命周期，涵盖从任务分配到执行监控再到技能复用的全过程。其关键特性包括：将智能体视为队友，它们拥有个人资料、出现在任务看板上、主动发表评论、创建问题并报告障碍；支持自主执行，提供完整的任务生命周期管理（排队、认领、开始、完成/失败），并通过WebSocket进行实时进度流传输；强调可重用技能，每个解决方案都能转化为整个团队可复用的技能，如部署、迁移、代码审查等，从而随时间推移不断增强团队的整体能力。，该平台被设计为管理式智能体的开源基础设施，具有供应商中立、可自托管的特点，专为人与AI协同工作的团队打造。它兼容多种AI模型，包括Claude Code、Codex、OpenClaw和OpenCode。项目提供了云服务、自托管选项以及详细的贡献指南，其开源代码托管于GitHub。

The Problems From Andrej's post: "The models make wrong assumptions on your behalf and just run along with them without checking. They don't manage their confusion, don't seek clarifications, don't surface inconsistencies, don't present tradeoffs, don't push back when they should." "They really like to overcomplicate code and APIs, bloat abstractions, don't clean up dead code... implement a bloated construction over 1000 lines when 100 would do." "They still sometimes change/remove comments and code they don't sufficiently understand as side effects, even if orthogonal to the task." The Solution Four principles in one file that directly address these issues: Principle Addresses Think Before Coding Wrong assumptions, hidden confusion, missing tradeoffs Simplicity First Overcomplication, bloated abstractions Surgical Changes Orthogonal edits, touching code you shouldn't Goal-Driven Execution Leverage through tests-first, verifiable success criteria The Four Principles in Detail 1. Think Before Coding Don't assume. Don't hide confusion. Surface tradeoffs. LLMs often pick an interpretation silently and run with it. This principle forces explicit reasoning: State assumptions explicitly — If uncertain, ask rather than guess Present multiple interpretations — Don't pick silently when ambiguity exists Push back when warranted — If a simpler approach exists, say so Stop when confused — Name what's unclear and ask for clarification 2. Simplicity First Minimum code that solves the problem. Nothing speculative. Combat the tendency toward overengineering: No features beyond what was asked No abstractions for single-use code No "flexibility" or "configurability" that wasn't requested No error handling for impossible scenarios If 200 lines could be 50, rewrite it The test: Would a senior engineer say this is overcomplicated? If yes, simplify. 3. Surgical Changes Touch only what you must
title_zh: forrestchang/andrej-karpathy-skills - 一个单独的CLAUDE.md文件，用于改进Claude代码行为，源自Andrej Karpathy对LLM编码陷阱的观察。
summary_zh: GitHub用户forrestchang创建了一个名为"andrej-karpathy-skills"的项目，其核心是一个单独的CLAUDE.md文件，旨在优化Claude Code（Claude的代码生成功能）的行为。该项目灵感来源于AI研究员Andrej Karpathy对大型语言模型（LLM）在编程中常见缺陷的观察。，Karpathy指出LLM在编码时存在几个关键问题：模型会基于错误假设自行其是而不加验证；它们不管理自身的困惑感，不主动寻求澄清，不指出不一致之处，不呈现方案权衡，也不在必要时提出异议；此外，它们倾向于过度复杂化代码和API，引入臃肿的抽象层，不清理无用代码，甚至可能在不完全理解的情况下错误地修改或删除与任务无关的注释和代码。，针对这些问题，CLAUDE.md文件提出了四项核心原则来直接引导Claude Code的行为：，1. 先思考后编码：旨在解决错误假设和隐藏困惑的问题。要求模型明确陈述假设，在不确定时主动提问而非猜测；当存在歧义时，呈现多种解释而非 silently 选择；在存在更简单方案时提出异议；当感到困惑时，明确指出不清楚之处并寻求澄清。，2. 简洁至上：旨在对抗过度工程化和代码臃肿。要求代码只实现被要求的功能，不为单次使用的代码创建抽象，不添加未请求的“灵活性”或“可配置性”，不为不可能发生的场景添加错误处理。核心测试标准是：一位资深工程师是否会认为这过于复杂？如果是，则进行简化。，3. 精准修改：旨在防止无关的代码改动。要求模型只修改必须修改的部分，避免触及与当前任务正交的代码。，4. 目标驱动执行：旨在通过测试优先和可验证的成功标准来提升效率。，该项目通过一个集中的指导文件，将这些原则注入Claude Code的工作流程，以期生成更可靠、更简洁、更专注的代码，减少LLM常见的臆断和过度设计倾向。

title_zh: 港大/DeepTutor - "DeepTutor：原生智能体驱动的个性化学习助手
summary_zh: 香港大学数据科学实验室（HKUDS）推出的DeepTutor项目是一个“智能体原生”的个性化学习助手，近期发布了其里程碑式的v1.0.0版本。该项目在GitHub上已获得超过1.5万颗星标，显示出极高的社区关注度。，DeepTutor的核心在于其完全重构的“智能体原生”架构。它并非简单的聊天机器人，而是构建了一个由多个专门化AI智能体协同工作的系统，旨在模拟人类导师的个性化教学流程。项目提供了多种交互方式，包括名为TutorBot的对话式助手和一个命令行界面（CLI），并支持通过Docker进行便捷部署。，在技术特性上，DeepTutor展现出强大的功能与灵活性。它支持切换不同的底层大语言模型（LLM），如OpenAI的GPT系列和Anthropic的Claude模型（包括新支持的o4-mini），并采用了原生的SDK提供商以提升稳定性。系统具备“可视化”能力，通过Chart.js/SVG渲染管道生成图表；其“引导式学习”模块支持数学公式（KaTeX）渲染和课程导航。此外，它还集成了防止测验题目重复的生成历史记录、对LLM输出的鲁棒性JSON解析，以及完整的国际化（i18n）支持，尤其完善了对中文的覆盖。，该项目的价值在于提供了一个开源、可扩展的框架，用于构建真正个性化、交互式的AI辅导体验。它允许开发者基于Apache 2.0许可证进行自由使用和二次开发，旨在共同塑造AI教育的未来。社区通过Discord、微信和GitHub Discussions进行活跃交流，持续推动项目演进，最新版本已修复了跨平台依赖、缓存热重载等一系列问题，提升了整体稳定性和用户体验。

title_zh: opendataloader-project/opendataloader-pdf - 面向AI就绪数据的PDF解析器。自动化PDF可访问性。开源。
summary_zh: 开源项目 OpenDataLoader PDF 是一款专为 AI 准备数据的 PDF 解析器，旨在自动化处理 PDF 的可访问性。该项目在 GitHub 上发布，核心目标是高效、准确地将 PDF 内容转换为适合 AI 模型（如用于 RAG 应用）处理的格式，并推动 PDF 文档的无障碍化。，其核心功能与技术特点包括：首先，在数据提取方面表现卓越。该解析器能够从任何 PDF 中提取 Markdown、带边界框的 JSON 以及 HTML 格式的内容。根据基准测试，其在包含多栏排版和科学论文在内的 200 份真实世界 PDF 上的综合准确率达到 0.907，表格提取准确率高达 0.928，位列榜首。它提供两种处理模式：确定性的本地模式，以及用于处理复杂页面的 AI 混合模式。，其次，该工具具备强大的复杂内容处理能力。它支持扫描版 PDF 和 OCR（光学字符识别），在混合模式下内置支持 80 多种语言的 OCR 引擎，可处理分辨率在 300 DPI 及以上的低质量扫描件。对于复杂表格（包括无边框表格）、LaTeX 公式、图片和图表，混合模式能够提供 AI 生成的描述。，第三，该项目简化了与 AI 工作流的集成。用户可通过 `pip install opendataloader-pdf` 快速安装，仅用三行代码即可完成转换。其输出的结构化 Markdown 便于文本分块，带边界框的 JSON 便于追溯引用来源，HTML 则便于预览。同时，它提供了 Python、Node.js 和 Java 的 SDK，并支持与 LangChain 集成。，最后，项目致力于 PDF 无障碍访问的自动化。其布局分析引擎同样驱动着自动标记功能，目标是成为首个端到端生成“标记 PDF”的开源工具（计划于 2026 年第二季度发布），以应对全球日益严格的无障碍法规。该功能将遵循 Apache 2.0 协议免费开源，实现“未标记 PDF 输入，标记 PDF 输出”，且不依赖任何专有 SDK，未来还将支持向 PDF/UA 标准转换。

summary_zh: GitHub项目“claude-code-best-practice”是一个专注于提升Claude代码助手使用效率的最佳实践指南库，其核心理念是“实践使Claude臻于完美”。该项目旨在帮助开发者从基础的“氛围编码”过渡到更高级的“智能体工程”。，项目通过一套清晰的结构化概念来组织Claude Code的功能。核心组件包括：Subagents（子智能体），作为在独立上下文中运行的自主执行者，可配置自定义工具、权限、模型和记忆；Commands（命令），即注入到现有上下文中的知识，是用户可调用的简单提示模板，用于编排工作流；Skills（技能），同样是可注入的知识，但具备可配置、可预加载、可自动发现等特性，支持上下文分叉和渐进式披露。此外，项目还涵盖了Workflows（工作流编排）、Hooks（事件钩子）、MCP Servers（用于连接外部工具和API的模型上下文协议）、可分发的Plugins（插件包）、层级化的Settings（配置系统）、可定制的Status Line（状态栏）以及基于文件的Memory（记忆系统）。，项目强调实践性，提供了从教程、开发工作流、实现案例到演示报告在内的丰富资源。它支持自动化的Git-basedCheckpointing（检查点）功能，允许回退编辑，并提供了详细的CLI启动标志说明。该项目通过系统化的方法，展示了如何充分利用Claude Code的Agentic（智能体）能力，构建复杂、可维护的AI辅助编程工作流。
